One person. Ten applications. One architecture.

A local-first AI network for a whole life — built by a single human directing AI build chats under written law.

Read the source ↗ Open the system map →

Snapshot ▸ Jul 2026 10 apps core :8200 2,623 tests trading dry-run pre-company

This strip is a snapshot of the public repository, not a live feed. Nothing on this site polls anything.

Measured, not claimed

The numbers, each one click from its source

  • 10applications↗Source: the application table in the repository README
  • 2,600+commits since June 2026↗Source: the numbers table in the repository README
  • ~100,000lines of Python, across 633 files↗Source: the numbers table in the repository README
  • 2,623test functions in 309 files↗Source: the numbers table in the repository README

The published snapshot is one curated commit over 966 files — the 2,600+ commits are the working history behind it, and some modules are intentionally left out. We would rather say that than let a number imply something it doesn't.

What holds it together

Three decisions, everything else follows

Local-first

The core runs offline. Cloud services are opt-in connectors — always switchable off, always visible as an external step. That makes sync harder; we took the trade deliberately, because the data belongs on the machine that produced it.

Contract-driven

One written app contract, ten conforming applications. Meet it and the app appears in the dashboard by itself, reachable by the assistant. Write the contract, and the app appears.

Honest by law

Honesty is not a tone of voice here, it is a written rule the code has to obey: an unmeasured value is reported as unmeasured, never as a zero. The same rule governs this website.

The shape of it

A headless core, nine services around it

Every application owns its port, its database and its user interface. The core orchestrates and relays — it never writes outward on its own.

Open the system map → How the architecture works →

The ten

Each one runs alone. All ten speak one contract.

  • Dizzi-Core :8200AI orchestrator, panel registry, MCP hub, health watch.Read →
  • Dizz Money :8210Finance manager, ledger care-core, German tax filing.Read →
  • Dizz Communication :8218Unified inbox, smart contacts, appointment detection.Read →
  • Dizz Creating :8214Local media workbench over one shared GPU job queue.Read →
  • Dizz Memory :8212Markdown vault, full-text search, retrieval.Read →
  • Dizz Management :8213AI agent administration, autonomy ladder, network inbox.Read →
  • Dizz News :8216Sector-structured digest, cross-source dedup, watchlists.Read →
  • Dizz Healthy :8217Metrics, training, wearables — the most sensitive class.Read →
  • Dizz Admin :8222Vault, projects, business, studies, deadlines.Read →
  • Dizz Trading :8137Autonomous bot fleet with a hard human gate — dry-run.Read →
All ten in detail →

The method

Laws before code

A system this size is not buildable by one person without rules that hold when nobody is watching. These are written down, in the repository, and they are older than most of the code.

Law · honesty

“If a value was never measured, the system says so instead of showing a zero.”

Repository README, Principles↗Source: docs/01_GRUNDGESETZE.md
Law · human gates

“Nothing irreversible without a human. Money, publishing and deletion sit behind explicit gates. The trading fleet proposes; a person decides.”

Repository README, Principles↗Source: the Principles section of the repository README
Law · data at its source

“Data stays at its source; the Core only relays read-only; anything with outside effect needs an explicit human approval step.”

Repository README, on cross-connections↗Source: docs/26_QUERVERBINDUNGEN.md
How this actually gets built →

The chronicle

Dated, not narrated

  1. 8–25 June 2026

    Trading foundations and the validation nights

    Research protocols, Kelly sizing spec, system checks and dated night runs — the earliest documents in the repository.

  2. 12 June 2026

    The app contract

    The norm that turns nine separate services into one network: summary endpoint, MCP server, single sign-on, settings module.

  3. 3–11 July 2026

    Agent direction, export contract, chronicle core

    Autonomy ladder T0–T3 with an emergency stop, the single app as a sellable bundle, and a chronicle kernel with its own audit core.

  4. 23 July 2026

    Curated public snapshot

    966 files published as one commit. Source-visible, not open source.

The full timeline →

Independent project — incorporation ahead. We're opening early conversations with partners and investors.

From project to company →

Built with a 100-line Python baker. No cookies, no trackers, no external requests.
